verses.text: The righteous and wise are 'in the hand of God,' yet all share one outward lot, for 'one event' comes to the righteous and the wicked alike. Death falls on all. The believer's comfort is not exemption from it but God's keeping hand.

The righteous and wise are 'in the hand of God,' yet all share one outward lot, for 'one event' comes to the righteous and the wicked alike. Death falls on all. The believer's comfort is not exemption from it but God's keeping hand.

The righteous and wise are 'in the hand of God,' yet all share one outward lot, for 'one event' comes to the righteous and the wicked alike. Death falls on all. The believer's comfort is not exemption from it but God's keeping hand.

While there is life there is hope, for 'a living dog is better than a dead lion'; but the dead 'know not any thing' of life under the sun. Life is the time for action. Today's opportunities will not return once the grave closes.

While there is life there is hope, for 'a living dog is better than a dead lion'; but the dead 'know not any thing' of life under the sun. Life is the time for action. Today's opportunities will not return once the grave closes.

While there is life there is hope, for 'a living dog is better than a dead lion'; but the dead 'know not any thing' of life under the sun. Life is the time for action. Today's opportunities will not return once the grave closes.

So eat your bread with joy, wear white garments, and live happily with the wife you love, for God now accepts your works. Daily life is a gift to enjoy. The believer takes God's good things with a glad and thankful heart.

So eat your bread with joy, wear white garments, and live happily with the wife you love, for God now accepts your works. Daily life is a gift to enjoy. The believer takes God's good things with a glad and thankful heart.

So eat your bread with joy, wear white garments, and live happily with the wife you love, for God now accepts your works. Daily life is a gift to enjoy. The believer takes God's good things with a glad and thankful heart.

'Whatsoever thy hand findeth to do, do it with thy might,' for there is no work in the grave; yet 'time and chance happeneth to them all.' Make the most of life. Diligence is wise, though the outcome lies in God's hand.

'Whatsoever thy hand findeth to do, do it with thy might,' for there is no work in the grave; yet 'time and chance happeneth to them all.' Make the most of life. Diligence is wise, though the outcome lies in God's hand.

A poor wise man delivered a little city by his wisdom, yet no one remembered him; still 'wisdom is better than strength.' The world forgets its benefactors. Wisdom's worth is real even when it goes unthanked.

A poor wise man delivered a little city by his wisdom, yet no one remembered him; still 'wisdom is better than strength.' The world forgets its benefactors. Wisdom's worth is real even when it goes unthanked.

The quiet words of the wise are heard above a ruler of fools; 'wisdom is better than weapons of war: but one sinner destroyeth much good.' Wisdom outweighs force. Yet a single evil man can undo great good.
